name: Python Web Frameworks Research 2026
description: Search for the 5 most popular Python web frameworks in 2026, find their GitHub star counts, create a text-based bar chart ranking, and save to a markdown report

tasks.name: Search for popular Python web frameworks
tasks.description: You MUST call WebSearch to find the 5 most popular Python web frameworks in 2026. Use search query: 'most popular Python web frameworks 2026'. Output the FULL raw search results verbatim (URLs, titles, snippets). Do NOT summarize as 'results were found' — downstream tasks need the actual framework names and data. Output format: Include all search result titles, URLs, and snippet text.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains at least 5 distinct Python web framework names mentioned in search results with full URLs and snippets preserved

tasks.name: Find GitHub star counts
tasks.description: You MUST call WebSearch for each of the 5 Python web frameworks identified in task_001's output. For each framework, search for its GitHub repository and star count using queries like '<framework_name> GitHub stars 2026'. Output the FULL raw search results verbatim for ALL 5 frameworks (URLs, titles, snippets, star counts). Do NOT summarize. Cite task_001 as the source of framework names. If a star count is not found for a framework, say 'not found' for that framework. Output format: For each framework, include framework name, GitHub URL, and star count (or 'not found').
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains search results for 5 frameworks with GitHub URLs and star count information (or explicit 'not found' statements) that reference task_001's framework list

tasks.name: Create text-based bar chart
tasks.description: You MUST call python_exec to parse the GitHub star counts from task_002's output and create a text-based bar chart description ranking the 5 frameworks by star count. Write Python code that: 1) Extracts framework names and star counts from task_002 data, 2) Sorts them by star count descending, 3) Creates a text-based bar chart (using characters like '█' or '#' to represent relative popularity), 4) Outputs the ranked list with star counts and the visual chart. Do NOT invent star counts — only use data from task_002. If star counts are missing, note that in the output.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains Python code execution results showing 5 frameworks ranked by star count with a text-based visual bar chart representation

tasks.name: Save report to markdown file
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to save a comprehensive report to 'python_frameworks_report.md'. The report must include: 1) Title and introduction, 2) The 5 frameworks with their GitHub URLs and star counts from task_002, 3) The text-based bar chart ranking from task_003, 4) Sources/references from the web searches. Compile all data from previous tasks. Use markdown formatting for headers, lists, and code blocks.
tasks.success_criteria: File 'python_frameworks_report.md' is created containing framework names, GitHub star counts, text-based bar chart, and source citations
